mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of rising rate periods, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances following the 5.0% threshold crossing, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real new house sales confirms broad-based domestic demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in GDPC1 and RSAFS coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery phases, with directional accuracy below 55% or Rank IC < 0.02 in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent, markets anticipate aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods following the 2.5% inflation threshold, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal predicts negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction effect Rank IC drops below 0.02 across rolling 24-month evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising housing starts series indicates expanding residential construction activity and forward-looking economic confidence, which correlates with sustained corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST trends coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or the series fails to maintain a Rank IC above 0.03 in any 3-year rolling walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained expansions, rising industrial production reflects capacity bottlenecks and rising input costs rather than demand strength, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trends predict positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of supply-constrained regime samples, or the regime-filtered signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.
